name: Napoleonic Wars
description: The Napoleonic Wars ravaged Europe for over a decade, leaving millions dead and disrupting the early gains of the industrial revolution.

name: The Great Irish Famine
description: A catastrophic failure of the potato crop led to the death of one million Irish citizens and the emigration of another million, a wound still felt generations later.

name: The First World War
description: The Great War shattered the long peace of the 19th century, introducing industrial slaughter and destroying four empires. Over 20 million would die before it ended.

name: The Spanish Flu
description: In one terrible year, the influenza pandemic killed an estimated 50 million people worldwide, more than all combat deaths of the First World War.

name: The Second World War
description: The deadliest conflict in human history claimed over 70 million lives and ushered in the nuclear age. Civilization itself, for a time, seemed to hang in the balance.

name: The Great Chinese Famine
description: A combination of agricultural mismanagement and severe weather led to the deadliest famine in recorded history, claiming tens of millions of lives.

name: The HIV/AIDS Crisis
description: A new virus emerged that would eventually claim over 40 million lives, devastating communities across Africa and reshaping global public health institutions.
